verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (cysteine-rich secretory protein 1) is a secreted epididymal protein that binds non-covalently to the sperm surface during epididymal transit and is retained there into capacitation/fertilization. It has no TM domain or GPI anchor of its own, but the sperm-surface association is wash-resistant enough to persist through ejaculation and serve a documented role in sperm-egg interaction. This is a tissue/lineage-restricted post-translational attachment to a male germline cell surface acquired in a specialized secretory compartment (epididymis). Not cancer/cell-death induced; not lysosomal; not a TM cycling pool; the surface story is germline-restricted stable attachment to sperm.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Wash-resistance of CRISP1 sperm binding is partial — a loosely-bound pool elutes, while a tightly-bound pool persists; borderline between stable_surface_attachment and secreted_only.
